name: GA0: Design Sprint - Hand-Grading Rubric

parts.name: Individual — User Persona

criteria.name: Persona Realism & Specificity

checks.name: Realistic and specific
checks.description: Persona feels like a real person. Background and technical comfort level are specific and relevant to the assigned feature (not generic).

checks.name: Somewhat generic
checks.description: Persona exists but is generic (could apply to any feature) or lacks specificity in background/technical comfort.

checks.name: Missing or placeholder
checks.description: Persona is missing, a single sentence, or clearly a placeholder.

criteria.description: Persona is realistic, specific to the student's assigned feature, and includes name, background, and technical comfort level.

criteria.name: Goals & Pain Points

checks.name: Clear goals and pain points
checks.description: Goals are specific to CookYourBooks usage. Pain points reference real frustrations with existing tools or workflows. Both are clearly tied to the assigned feature.

checks.name: Goals or pain points are vague
checks.description: Goals or pain points are present but generic (e.g., "wants an easy app") rather than specific to the feature and CookYourBooks context.

checks.name: Missing goals or pain points
checks.description: Goals and/or pain points are missing entirely.

criteria.description: Persona includes clear goals (what they want to accomplish with CookYourBooks) and pain points (frustrations with existing solutions).

criteria.name: Usage Context

checks.name: Context provided
checks.description: Describes when/where/how the persona uses the app in a way that informs design decisions.

checks.name: Missing or trivial context
checks.description: No usage context, or context is trivial ("uses it at home").

criteria.description: Persona includes context: when, where, and how they use the app.

parts.description: A realistic persona (1 page) for a user who primarily uses the student's assigned core feature. Includes name, background, technical comfort level, goals, pain points, and context.

parts.name: Individual — Low-Fidelity Wireframes

criteria.name: Screen Coverage

checks.name: 3-5 screens with good coverage
checks.description: Wireframes show 3-5 distinct screens or states. The main view and important alternate states (e.g., loading, error, empty) are represented.

checks.name: 1-2 screens or incomplete coverage
checks.description: Only 1-2 screens, or screens are present but major states are missing.

checks.name: No wireframes
checks.description: No wireframes provided for this feature.

criteria.description: Wireframes include 3-5 screens covering the main view and key states of the assigned feature.

criteria.name: Interactions & State Changes

checks.name: Interactions clearly shown
checks.description: Wireframes annotate or illustrate what happens when the user clicks, types, or navigates. State transitions are visible (e.g., "click Save -> shows confirmation").

checks.name: Some interactions shown
checks.description: Some interactions are indicated but key ones are missing or unclear.

checks.name: No interactions shown
checks.description: Wireframes are static layouts with no indication of user interaction or state changes.

criteria.description: Wireframes show key user interactions and how the interface responds (state changes, transitions).

criteria.name: Cross-Feature Connections

checks.name: Connections clearly shown
checks.description: Wireframes indicate navigation to/from other features (e.g., "clicking a recipe navigates to Recipe Editor"). Entry and exit points are clear.

checks.name: Minimal connections
checks.description: Some reference to other features but connections are vague or incomplete.

checks.name: No cross-feature connections
checks.description: Feature is designed in isolation with no indication of how it connects to the rest of the app.

criteria.description: Wireframes show how this feature connects to or transitions from other features in the application.

parts.description: Hand-drawn or simple digital wireframes (3-5 screens) showing the main view, key interactions/state changes, and connections to other features.

parts.name: Individual — Accessibility Considerations

criteria.name: Keyboard & Screen Reader

checks.name: Both addressed
checks.description: Describes how the feature supports keyboard navigation (tab order, shortcuts) and what screen reader announcements are needed (labels, state changes).

checks.name: Missing or trivial
checks.description: Keyboard navigation and/or screen reader considerations are missing or only mentioned in passing without specifics.

criteria.description: Addresses keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements for the feature.

criteria.name: Color/Contrast & Visual Accessibility

checks.name: Addressed
checks.description: Describes how the feature will handle color/contrast for visual accessibility (e.g., not relying on color alone, contrast ratios).

checks.name: Missing or trivial
checks.description: Color/contrast not mentioned or only generic statement with no feature-specific detail.

criteria.description: Addresses color and contrast considerations for visual accessibility.

criteria.name: WCAG Reference

checks.name: WCAG referenced
checks.description: References specific WCAG guidelines (by number or name) and explains their relevance to this feature.

checks.name: No WCAG reference
checks.description: No reference to WCAG guidelines, or mentions WCAG only generically without connecting to the feature.

criteria.description: References specific WCAG guidelines relevant to the feature.

parts.description: Brief document (1/2 page) addressing keyboard navigation, screen reader announcements, color/contrast, and WCAG guidelines relevant to the assigned feature.

parts.name: Individual — Pull Request

criteria.name: Authored a PR

checks.name: PR authored
checks.description: At least one PR authored by this student is visible in the repository history.

checks.name: No PR found
checks.description: No pull request authored by this student was found in the repository.

criteria.description: Student authored at least one pull request in the team repository for this assignment.

parts.description: Each team member must author at least one pull request for this assignment.

criteria.name: Meeting 2: GA0 Design Walk-Through (Mar 30-31)

checks.name: Demonstrates comprehension
checks.description: Student can explain their design artifacts at both a high level and in detail, articulate design decisions, and describe their team's full design. No deduction.

checks.name: Partial comprehension
checks.description: Student can explain some aspects of the design but struggles with details, cross-feature connections, or team deliverables they didn't personally create. Half of maximum deduction applied.

checks.name: Cannot explain design
checks.description: Student cannot explain the design artifacts or design decisions. Full deduction applied.

checks.name: Absent without notice
checks.description: Student missed the meeting without prior notice to the TA. Full deduction applied.

criteria.description: Meeting 2 (Mar 30–31): Design sprint walk-through. Student explains their GA0 submission — architecture diagram, wireframes, and design decisions. Any team member may be asked about any team deliverable. Assess across: Code Comprehension (4), Process & Workflow (3), Collaboration Evidence (2), Forward Planning (1). Max deduction: -5 pts.

parts.name: Team — Architecture Diagram

criteria.name: ViewModel-Service Connections

checks.name: Clear and accurate
checks.description: Diagram clearly shows View -> ViewModel -> Service relationships. Each feature's ViewModel is mapped to the services it uses. Connections are accurate given the HW5 service layer.

checks.name: Present but incomplete or inaccurate
checks.description: Diagram exists but is missing some connections, has inaccurate service mappings, or does not clearly show the View-ViewModel-Service layering.

checks.name: Missing or unusable
checks.description: No architecture diagram, or diagram is too vague to be useful (e.g., a single box labeled "app").

criteria.description: Diagram shows how Views connect to ViewModels and how ViewModels connect to the existing service layer from HW5.

criteria.name: New Components & Clarity

checks.name: Clear with new components identified
checks.description: Diagram is readable, well-labeled, and identifies any new components or shared utilities the team plans to build.

checks.name: Unclear or missing new components
checks.description: Diagram is hard to read, unlabeled, or does not identify planned new components.

criteria.description: Diagram identifies any new components the team plans to add and is generally clear and readable.

parts.description: Diagram showing how UI screens connect to HW5 services, the View-ViewModel-Service relationships, and any new components.

parts.name: Team — Integrated Wireframes

criteria.name: Navigation Flow

checks.name: Navigation flow shown
checks.description: Clear navigation paths between features are illustrated (arrows, annotations, or flow diagram). Covers all implemented features.

checks.name: Navigation flow missing or unclear
checks.description: Navigation between features is not shown or is too vague to understand.

criteria.description: Document shows how users navigate between the implemented features.

criteria.name: Shared UI Elements

checks.name: Shared elements identified
checks.description: Shared elements (header, nav bar, common patterns) are called out and shown consistently across feature wireframes.

checks.name: No shared elements identified
checks.description: Individual wireframes are combined but no shared elements are identified or discussed.

criteria.description: Document identifies shared UI elements (header, navigation bar, common button styles, etc.).

criteria.name: Cross-Feature Design Decisions

checks.name: Decisions documented
checks.description: At least one cross-feature design decision is documented (e.g., how recipe selection in Library triggers Editor, consistent error display pattern). 3-person teams state which feature was omitted.

checks.name: No cross-feature decisions
checks.description: No cross-feature design decisions discussed, or 3-person team does not state which feature was omitted.

criteria.description: Document notes design decisions that affect multiple features. 3-person teams explicitly state which feature was omitted.

parts.description: Combined document (design/integrated-wireframes.md) showing navigation flow between features, shared UI elements, and cross-feature design decisions.

parts.name: Team — User-Facing Terminology

criteria.name: Terminology Consistency

checks.name: Comprehensive and consistent
checks.description: Terminology covers all major UI elements across implemented features. Mappings make sense from a user perspective (e.g., "RecipeCollection" -> "Cookbook"). 3-person teams note omitted feature.

checks.name: Partial coverage
checks.description: Some terminology defined but missing terms for major elements, or terms are inconsistent across features.

checks.name: Missing or minimal
checks.description: No terminology document, or only 1-2 trivial entries.

criteria.description: Team has established consistent user-facing terms for all major UI elements across implemented features.

parts.description: Consistent terminology mapping from code/domain terms to user-facing labels for all major UI elements in the implemented features.

parts.name: Team — Feature Buffet Selection

criteria.name: Selection & Count

checks.name: 2-3 features selected
checks.description: Team selected 2-3 features from the Standard or Advanced Feature Buffet lists.

checks.name: Wrong count or not from buffet
checks.description: Fewer than 2 or more than 3 features, or features not from the provided lists.

criteria.description: Team selected 2-3 features from the Feature Buffet.

criteria.name: Rationale & Persona Connection

checks.name: Strong rationale for all selections
checks.description: Each feature has 2-3 sentences explaining why it was chosen, connects to at least one persona, and notes any anticipated concerns or risks.

checks.name: Partial rationale
checks.description: Some features have rationale but others are missing justification, persona connection, or risk assessment.

checks.name: No rationale
checks.description: Features are listed but with no explanation of why they were chosen.

criteria.description: Each selected feature has a brief rationale explaining why it was chosen, which persona(s) benefit, and anticipated risks.

parts.description: 2-3 features selected from the Feature Buffet with rationale tied to personas. Documented in design/buffet-selection.md.

parts.name: Team — "Our Feature" Concept

criteria.name: Creativity & User Need

checks.name: Creative with clear user need
checks.description: Feature is genuinely original (not on the Buffet), addresses a real problem, identifies benefiting persona(s), and describes how users currently work around not having it. Scope is at least as substantial as a Buffet item.

checks.name: Present but weak
checks.description: Feature exists but is trivial in scope, duplicates a Buffet item, or the user need is not convincingly argued.

checks.name: Missing
checks.description: No "Our Feature" concept provided.

criteria.description: Feature is creative (not on the Buffet), addresses a real user need, and identifies which persona(s) benefit.

criteria.name: Design Concept & Wireframes

checks.name: Well-designed with wireframes
checks.description: Includes 3-5 wireframe screens, describes key interactions, and explains integration with existing features.

checks.name: Missing or minimal design
checks.description: No wireframes, or only a text description with no visual design artifacts.

criteria.description: Includes wireframes/sketches (3-5 screens), key user interactions, and how it integrates with existing features.

criteria.name: Technical Considerations & Honest Assessment

checks.name: Technical depth and honest assessment
checks.description: Discusses what services/APIs are needed, estimates complexity, and gives an honest 2-3 sentence explanation of why the team didn't build it (scope, time, dependencies).

checks.name: Missing technical or assessment section
checks.description: Technical considerations or "why we didn't build it" section is missing or trivial.

criteria.description: Includes technical considerations (services/APIs needed, complexity, architectural changes) and an honest assessment of why it wasn't built.

parts.description: Custom feature NOT on the Feature Buffet, documented in design/our-feature.md. Includes feature name/tagline, user need, design concept with wireframes, technical considerations, and honest assessment of why it wasn't built.

description: Manual grading rubric for GA0: Design Sprint (30 points total). 15 points individual (persona, wireframes, accessibility, PR) + 15 points team (architecture, integrated wireframes, terminology, buffet selection, "Our Feature" concept).
